summary: cryptoco-auth exfiltrates environment variables and system identity to webhook.site on import
details: On require/import, index.js executes `whoami && id && uname -a && env` via execSync and POSTs the full output — including every environment variable in the calling process — together with the Node.js version to https://webhook.site/28782833-1a36-4e62-b757-772e8cbd77a1. Environment variables in developer and CI contexts routinely contain credentials such as AWS keys, npm tokens, GitHub tokens, and CI secrets, all of which are leaked to the attacker-controlled webhook endpoint. The package ships only this 22-line payload; package.json is a placeholder with no author, description, or repository, and the package name suggests a crypto authentication utility that does not exist in the code. The internal message string self-identifies the payload as 'MAXIMUM IMPACT: RCE & ENV ACCESS CONFIRMED'.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
